title: SUSPENSION UNIT FOR CONVEYOR BELT
abstract: The disclosure relates to a suspension unit for bearing material load impact on a conveyor belt. The suspension unit includes an impact bearing member, such as a roller or a plate fabricated from a low friction material, mounted on an upper support member, a lower portion comprising a lower support member configured, in use, to be mounted on a frame for supporting the conveyor belt, and one or more resilient members. The resilient members engage the upper and lower support members in a manner to allow the upper support member to be displaced under load with respect to the lower support member.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A suspension unit for bearing material load impact on a conveyor belt, the suspension unit comprising: an upper portion comprising an impact bearing member mounted on an upper support member; a lower portion comprising a lower support member configured, in use, to be mounted on a frame for supporting a conveyor belt; and one or more resilient members, wherein the resilient members engage the upper and lower support members in a manner to allow the upper support member to be displaced under load with respect to the lower support member. 2. The suspension unit according to claim 1, wherein the impact bearing member comprises a roller. 3. The suspension unit according to claim 1, wherein the impact bearing member comprises a plate having an upper surface fabricated from a low friction material. 4. The suspension unit according to claim 1, wherein the impact bearing member comprises a plate fabricated from a low friction material. 5. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the resilient member comprises a torsional spring. 6. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the resilient member comprises a block of a compressible material. 7. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the upper support member comprises an upper bracket and the lower support member comprises a lower bracket. 8. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the upper support member comprises an elongate upper bracket and the lower support member comprises a pair of spaced apart lower brackets. 9. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the upper support member comprises an upper bracket or an elongate bracket and the lower support member comprises a shaft. 10. The suspension unit according to claim 8 or claim 9, wherein a plurality of plates are mounted on the elongate upper bracket in adjacent alignment with one another. 11. The suspension unit according to claim 10, wherein the elongate upper bracket is provided with one or more upwardly inclined wing members extending outwardly from opposing side walls of the elongate upper bracket, wherein said wing members are adapted in use to have respective plates mounted thereon. 12. The suspension unit according to claim 11, wherein the elongate upper bracket is provided with two pairs of spaced apart upwardly inclined wing members. 13. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 7, 8, 11 or 12, wherein said upper bracket or said elongate upper bracket is configured to interchangeably receive the impact bearing member or the roller. 14. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 1 to 8, 10 to 13, wherein the lower bracket has a lower surface configured to contiguously abut an upper surface of a conveyor belt frame member. 15. The suspension unit according to claim 14, wherein the lower bracket is provided with a clip to engage the lower bracket with said frame member. 16. The suspension unit according to claim 14, wherein the lower bracket is fixed to said frame member. 17. The suspension unit according to claim 14, wherein said frame member is provided with slots and the lower bracket is provided with respective flanges disposed, in use, to be received in the slots, thereby fixedly mounting the lower bracket on said frame member. 18. The suspension unit according to claim 9, wherein opposing ends of the shaft are configured to be received in respective recesses provided in said frame, such that the shaft remains fixed with respect to rotational movement. 19. The suspension unit according to claim 9 or claim 18, wherein at least a portion of the shaft is encompassed by the compressible material, and the compressible material is mounted to an underside of a mounting plate of the upper bracket or the upper elongate bracket with a C-bracket. 20. The suspension unit according to claim 19, wherein the upper bracket or the upper elongate bracket is displaced substantially vertically under load with respect to the shaft. 21. The suspension unit according to claim 9 or claim 18, wherein at least a portion of the shaft is encompassed by the compressible material, and the compressible material is in operational communication with a torsional spring engaged by the upper bracket or the upper elongate bracket in an arrangement whereby the upper bracket or the upper elongate bracket is angularly displaced under load with respect to the shaft. 22. The suspension unit according to any one of claims 3 to 21, wherein the plate is fixed to the upper bracket by fasteners manufactured from a low friction material. 23. The suspension unit according to claim 22, wherein the impact bearing member comprises a retainer to prevent the plate moving in a direction of travel of the conveyor belt. 24. A impact bearing member for a suspension unit configured for bearing material load impact on a conveyor belt, said impact bearing member comprises a plate fabricated from a low friction material or a plate having an upper surface fabricated from a low friction material, wherein the plate is provided with a plurality of apertures to allow the impact bearing member to be fixed to said suspension unit by a plurality of fasteners, and a plurality of retainers to prevent the plate moving in a direction of travel of the conveyor belt. 25. A conveyor belt system comprising a plurality of suspension units as defined in any one of claims 1 to 23. 26. The conveyor belt system according to claim 25, wherein the plurality of suspension units are arranged in an alternating array of suspension units comprising plates fabricated from a low friction material or plates having an upper surface fabricated from a low friction material and suspension units comprising rollers.
